segments.caption: The video opens with a vibrant, animated title screen featuring the text 'Let’s go' surrounded by a radiant burst of colorful, flowing lines, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo in the corner. The scene transitions to a close-up of two individuals high-fiving, overlaid with the same colorful animation, suggesting excitement and collaboration. The setting shifts to a modern, spacious convention center where four hosts—two men and two women—stand together, each holding a 'Cisco Live!' branded microphone, smiling and engaging with the camera. The camera then pans to an aerial view of a historic cityscape with prominent church spires, followed by a quick shot of the exterior of a large convention center adorned with 'Cisco Live!' banners. Inside, attendees are seen at a 'Badge Collection' counter, receiving their event credentials. The video cuts to a host, Steve Multer, speaking into a microphone with a dynamic, colorful lower-third graphic identifying him as a Cisco Live Host. The scene transitions to a presentation in the 'DevNet Theater,' where a speaker demonstrates code on a large screen. Next, attendees interact with a Cisco representative at a booth, using a laptop to explore Cisco’s offerings. The video then shows a presentation on network infrastructure, with a large screen displaying diagrams of Cisco products like 'Cisco Silicon One™' and 'Cisco Optics.' Attendees are seen engaged in discussions, with one man gesturing animatedly while speaking to a woman. The video concludes with close-ups of two hosts, Nish Parker and Cedric De Vylder, each introduced with lower-third graphics identifying them as Cisco Live Hosts, speaking enthusiastically to the camera in the bustling event environment. The overall atmosphere is energetic, professional, and focused on networking, technology, and collaboration at the Cisco Live! event in Amsterdam, February 5–8, 2024.

segments.caption: The video opens with a dynamic shot of a male speaker on stage at a Cisco Live! event, gesturing toward a large screen displaying a diagram, with a robot figure visible in the background. The scene quickly transitions to a vibrant, colorful graphic with the text 'World of Solutions' before cutting to a close-up of a man with a beard, identified as Robb Boyd, a Cisco Live Host, speaking directly to the camera. The camera then pans across the bustling convention floor, capturing attendees interacting with various booths, including one labeled 'Cisco Showcase' and another with a 'Welcome' sign. A woman in a green dress, Nish Parker, is seen holding a 'Cisco Live!' microphone, engaging with the audience. The video continues with quick cuts through different zones of the event, including the 'Meeting Village' and 'Innovation Theater,' where a male host in a dark suit speaks on stage. The DevNet Zone is highlighted with a large sign, followed by scenes of attendees networking and engaging with technology demonstrations, including a presentation on HVAC monitoring. The segment concludes with a close-up of a smiling woman with glasses, laughing during a conversation, emphasizing the interactive and lively atmosphere of the event.

segments.caption: The video opens with Steve Multer, a male host in a dark jacket, speaking energetically into a 'Cisco Live!' microphone in front of a vibrant green and yellow backdrop with the 'DevNet Zone' sign visible. The scene quickly transitions to a wide shot of the bustling convention center, showcasing attendees walking past booths labeled 'Sustainability Design Clinic' and 'Internet for the Future,' with a large screen displaying 'Security' in the background. The camera then cuts to a close-up of a Cisco-branded backpack, followed by a sweeping view of the expansive 'Innovation Theater' with rows of empty seats and large screens displaying 'Innovation Talks' and 'Capture the Benefits of Customer Experience and AI.' Cedric De Vylder, in a blue jacket, appears next, holding a microphone and speaking in front of the same theater setup. The video then shows a behind-the-scenes shot of a camera operator filming a presenter, followed by Robb Boyd, another male host with a beard, speaking into a microphone in a studio-like setting. The scene shifts to Nish Parker, a female host in a green dress, standing beside Cedric De Vylder in front of a 'Social Media Central' sign, both holding microphones. A lower-third graphic encourages viewers to 'Join the conversation at #CiscoLiveEMEA.' The video concludes with a group of four hosts—two men and two women—standing together in the convention center, smiling and holding microphones, followed by a vibrant animated graphic with the text 'Let’s go' in bold, colorful letters, signaling the start of the event.

segments.caption: The video opens with a vibrant, abstract transition featuring colorful, flowing shapes before settling into a wide shot of a modern, brightly lit convention center stage at Cisco Live!. Two male hosts are positioned behind separate white podiums with colorful geometric panels—yellow and orange—set against a backdrop of vertical wooden slats illuminated with blue LED lighting. The host on the left, wearing a dark blazer over a black shirt, gestures animatedly while speaking, occasionally leaning forward and using hand motions to emphasize points. He has a laptop with a rainbow-colored screen cover and a small black device on his podium. The host on the right, dressed in a light gray zip-up sweater, listens attentively, occasionally smiling and nodding, with a silver MacBook and a black mug on his podium. The camera alternates between wide shots of both hosts and closer shots focusing on each individually. In the background, signage for 'Cisco Learning', 'World of Solutions', 'Cisco Showcase', 'The Hub Hall 7', and 'Meeting Village' is visible, along with tables, stools, and green plants, indicating a lively, interactive event space. The atmosphere is professional yet dynamic, with the hosts engaging in a conversational presentation, likely discussing Cisco technologies or event highlights. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently displayed in the top right corner of the screen.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, standing on a dark stage with a deep blue backdrop. He is wearing a dark blazer over a gray and white plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, and he speaks with expressive hand gestures, pointing and emphasizing his points. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is visible in the top right corner. The camera then pulls back to reveal a wide, circular stage with a large, curved video screen above it, displaying the speaker’s image from multiple angles. The screen shows a presentation slide with the text 'Cisco is the Internet data company with billions of daily measurements' alongside a dynamic image of a cityscape. The stage is illuminated with vertical neon light strips in vibrant colors—purple, blue, red, and green—creating a high-tech, immersive atmosphere. The audience, seated in rows, is visible in the dimly lit auditorium, some clapping and others recording with smartphones. The speaker continues to move across the stage, gesturing toward the screen as he announces 'ThousandEyes with Cisco Secure Access,' which appears in bold white text on a blue and yellow gradient background. The presentation then transitions to a detailed dashboard titled 'Experience Insights Powered by ThousandEyes,' showing real-time data on endpoints, including user names, locations, health status, latency, jitter, loss, CPU, and memory usage. The dashboard highlights several endpoints, some marked as 'Healthy' with green checkmarks, while others are 'Offline.' The screen also displays a 'Config' button and a timestamp indicating the data was last updated on December 13, 2023. The speaker continues to explain the features, pointing to the screen as the camera alternates between wide shots of the stage and close-ups of the dashboard, emphasizing the integration of ThousandEyes technology for monitoring user, application, and network performance.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a user navigating a configuration interface titled 'Monitor Critical Applications for SD-WAN,' selecting a network named 'MX Branch' to activate as a ThousandEyes agent for application testing. The interface displays a step-by-step wizard with options to connect ThousandEyes, select applications like Salesforce, and configure monitoring. After selecting the network, the user proceeds to a summary screen confirming the activation of the network as an agent, with a 'Start monitoring' button visible. The scene transitions to a dashboard listing 26 Enterprise Agents, each with an agent name (e.g., MX-Branch-1), hostname, utilization percentage, and status/last contact time, indicating real-time network health. The video then shifts to a detailed path visualization graph showing network traces from multiple agents to a target server (https://abc.salesforce.com/), with nodes color-coded by status (red for errors, green for success). The graph highlights a specific node, 'xe-3-3-2-dcr1-lon.net.sfdc.net,' with a pop-up displaying metrics such as 84% forwarding loss, 73.3 ms average response, and location in London, England, UK. The final frames show a split-screen view with the path visualization on the left and a male presenter on the right, identified as Jonathan Davidson, speaking on stage at Cisco Live! He is wearing a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, gesturing expressively as he explains the network diagnostics. The stage features dynamic lighting with vertical neon strips in purple and blue, and the Cisco Live! logo is prominently displayed in the top right corner. The presentation slide behind him reads 'Simplifying and securing networking at every scale,' reinforcing the theme of advanced network monitoring and troubleshooting.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a presentation by Jonathan Davidson, EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, who is seen on stage in a dark blazer over a blue plaid shirt, gray trousers, and dark sneakers, gesturing expressively as he speaks. The screen displays a 'Packet Capture' interface from the Meraki Dashboard, showing fields for switch selection, ports, duration, filter expressions, and a 'Start capture' button, with sample filter expressions listed. The scene transitions to the 'Ports' tab of the Meraki Dashboard, showing a diagram of switch ports with status indicators and historical device data graphs for connectivity and client usage. Next, the 'Config History' tab is shown, displaying configuration changes with timestamps and a toggle for 'Compare changes'. The presentation then moves to the 'Firmware upgrades' section, where a 'Schedule upgrade' dialog is opened, showing a 'New version' dropdown and a 'Schedule Upgrade' button. After scheduling, a 'Success' notification confirms the upgrade is pending. The view shifts to the 'Switches' list, showing device statuses, MAC addresses, and firmware versions, with two switches marked as 'Alerting'. The 'Add devices' interface is shown, allowing claims by serial or order number. The segment continues with a 'Usage and clients' dashboard, displaying a graph of network usage over time and a list of connected clients with details like device type, OS, and MAC address. Finally, the 'Wireless-Air' client details are shown, including a map of the client's location, current connection diagram, and usage graph, with the presenter continuing to explain the features. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, featuring a circular stage with a speaker standing center stage, illuminated by blue stage lights and surrounded by a massive audience. The speaker, a man in a dark blazer over a plaid shirt and gray trousers, gestures expressively as he presents. The large curved video screen above the stage displays his image and transitions to a software interface for Cisco Secure Access, showing policy configurations and credential input. The interface highlights the 'Cisco Secure Access Credential' dialog box, where an API key and secret are entered, followed by a 'Save' action. The screen then shows the 'Policies' page with a 'US-West Branches' policy group, where the 'Embedded Security' is set to 'Cisco_Secure_Access_Connectivity'. The speaker clicks 'Deploy', and a 'Success' notification appears, confirming the policy was deployed to 20 sites. The scene transitions to a vibrant, colorful graphic with the text 'Let’s go' and the Cisco Live! logo, before returning to the stage where the speaker continues his presentation, now with a large screen displaying a multi-panel security dashboard with live surveillance footage. The stage lighting includes vertical neon strips in red, blue, and green, enhancing the high-tech atmosphere. The speaker moves dynamically across the stage, engaging the audience with animated gestures, while the large screens display his image from multiple angles, emphasizing the immersive presentation environment.

segments.caption: The video begins with a detailed view of a security monitoring interface, specifically the Cisco Meraki Vision dashboard, displaying a grid of surveillance thumbnails from a train station on Friday, December 15th. The interface includes filters for time, cameras, people (with color-coded top and bottom clothing options), and vehicles (with type and color filters), allowing for refined event searches. The thumbnails show various scenes: people waiting on platforms, trains arriving, individuals walking through turnstiles, and a person near the tracks. The scene then transitions to a live presentation at Cisco Live!, where Jonathan Davidson, identified as EVP and General Manager of Cisco Networking, stands on a dark stage with vibrant vertical neon light strips in purple, blue, red, and green. He gestures expressively while speaking to a large audience, with his image projected on a large curved screen above the stage. The presentation returns to the Meraki Vision interface, where filters are adjusted to focus on people, including a slider for 'Object 1' from 'Rough match' to 'Exact match' and a 'Minimum event duration' slider. The video then cuts to a wide-angle surveillance view of a railway tunnel entrance with green safety mats laid on the tracks, followed by a return to the stage where the speaker continues his presentation. The final segment shows a split-screen view of the Meraki Vision interface in 'List View' mode, highlighting a specific event where a person in an orange jacket is seen on the train tracks, demonstrating the system’s ability to detect and flag potential safety incidents. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment, reinforcing the event’s branding.

As Jeetu continues to gesture and explain, the diagram evolves: a red warning icon appears next to the 'Marketing app', followed by a red dotted line connecting it to the 'Customer DB' in the 'Prod' section, indicating a security threat or data breach. The red lines then expand to show connections to multiple other components within the private cloud, visually demonstrating the potential spread of the threat. The stage lighting remains focused on Jeetu, with spotlights and the vibrant neon strips enhancing the high-tech atmosphere of the keynote presentation.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where a speaker, identified as Jeetu Patel, stands on a stage illuminated by vertical blue and purple neon light strips. He is bald, wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, holding a small black device. The audience is seated in rows, many wearing lanyards, and the stage features a large screen displaying the speaker’s image. The scene transitions to a close-up of a software interface titled 'AI Assistant,' which displays a blue circular logo and prompts the user with 'What do you want to ask today?' with several suggested questions like 'Access Control Rule Summary' and 'Schedule Recurring Backups: A How-To Guide.' The speaker gestures expressively as he explains the interface, and the screen shows a user typing a query: 'I need to update our firewall policy. What policies are controlling the Sales APP?' The AI Assistant responds with a list of access control policies, including 'Application_Access' and 'Edge_Control_Application,' detailing their status and last modification dates. The speaker continues to explain, and the interface then shows a new query: 'Add a rule to Application_Access that blocks outbound traffic from SalesApp,' followed by a suggestion to block all outbound traffic from SalesApp with options to 'Yes' or 'No.' The speaker remains engaged, gesturing and speaking into a microphone, while the interface displays the AI Assistant’s response. The background features dynamic, colorful abstract shapes, and the 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner.

segments.caption: The video segment features Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, presenting on a stage with dynamic blue and purple neon lighting. He holds a small black device and gestures expressively while speaking. On the main screen, an AI Assistant interface is displayed, showing a suggestion to block outbound traffic from a 'SalesApp' for all users, with options to create the rule. After a 'Yes' selection, a confirmation message appears: 'Congratulations, Block-Outbound-SalesApp rule has been successfully created!' The assistant then asks if further help is needed, and a new query is typed: 'Give me step-by-step instructions to upgrade the firewall.' The assistant responds with a detailed nine-step guide for upgrading the Cisco Secure Firewall, including logging into the Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC), navigating to device management, selecting the upgrade version, copying the upgrade package, monitoring progress, and running a readiness check. The stage environment includes vertical neon light strips and a marked stage area, with the Cisco Live! branding visible in the top right corner. segments.caption: The video segment begins with a close-up of an AI Assistant interface, displaying step-by-step instructions for upgrading a firewall via the Cisco Secure Firewall Management Center (FMC). In a smaller inset on the right, Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses and a dark blazer over a black shirt, is seen on stage holding a small black device, gesturing as he speaks. The scene then transitions to a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium filled with an audience, illuminated by blue stage lights. Jeetu Patel is now visible on a large central screen, continuing his presentation. The camera returns to a close-up of the AI Assistant interface, where a notification for 'Policy Analysis and Optimization' appears, revealing that 323 duplicate rules (153 shadowed, 170 redundant) were found out of 1234 total rules. The interface offers 'Disable all' and 'Delete all' buttons. After the 'Delete all' action is selected, a confirmation message states 'All 323 duplicate rules have been disabled,' followed by a prompt asking if the user wants to deploy the changes to 4 devices. The user selects 'Yes,' and the interface confirms with 'Congrats, all changes have been deployed!' The entire sequence demonstrates the AI Assistant's capability to automate firewall policy optimization and deployment, with Jeetu Patel guiding the audience through the process on stage.

segments.caption: The segment opens with Jeetu Patel, a bald man wearing clear-framed glasses, a dark blazer over a black shirt, and dark trousers, standing on a dimly lit stage with dynamic blue and purple neon lighting. He holds a small black device in his hand and gestures expressively as he speaks, occasionally adjusting his earpiece. The camera cuts to a presentation slide displaying a colorful, overlapping grid of technology company logos including Microsoft, Cisco, Okta, Google, Amazon, Salesforce, CrowdStrike, Zscaler, CyberArk, PingIdentity, SailPoint, and Dragos, set against a dark blue background with abstract light streaks. The slide transitions to a diagram titled 'Cisco Identity Intelligence,' showing the same logos now uniformly blue, with arrows pointing upward to icons labeled 'Users,' 'Machines,' 'Services,' 'Apps,' 'Data,' and 'Behaviors.' The scene then shifts to a full-stage view of Jeetu Patel, who continues to speak and gesture while standing in front of the large screen displaying the 'Cisco Identity Intelligence' diagram. The presentation then transitions to a complex 'IDENTITY GRAPH' visualization, illustrating interconnected nodes for Users, Machines, Services, Data, and Platforms, with labels such as HRIS, Okta, AWS, and SaaS, all connected by lines to form a network. The 'Cisco Live!' logo remains consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment, reinforcing the event branding.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a dark blue background displaying the title 'Cisco Identity Intelligence' and a complex network diagram labeled 'IDENTITY GRAPH,' which visually connects various digital entities such as Users, Machines, Services, Apps, Data, and Platforms through interconnected nodes and lines. The diagram includes icons and labels for HRIS, Okta, AWS, SaaS, and other systems, illustrating a comprehensive identity management ecosystem. As the segment progresses, the title changes to 'Example 1: Continuously assess behavior,' and the focus shifts to a specific user profile. A circular image of a man with glasses, identified as 'Jeetu Patel,' appears under the 'Users' node, with associated data including UID: 555192931, Role: 5550911, and Location: San Jose, CA. The user is linked to 'Jeetu’s Macbook Pro' under 'Machines,' which runs macOS 14.2.1. The 'Groups' node updates to include 'Exec Team' and later 'Database Admins,' indicating role changes. The 'Services' node shows a connection to 'Location: San Jose, CA,' and the 'Apps' node expands to include 'Microsoft Office 365,' 'Box,' and 'Oracle Customer DB,' with the latter highlighted in red, suggesting a new or critical access point. The visual narrative emphasizes real-time behavior assessment and dynamic identity updates within the Cisco Identity Intelligence framework, with the 'Cisco Live!' logo consistently displayed in the top right corner.

segments.caption: The video segment opens with a presentation slide titled 'Cisco Full-Stack Observability,' emphasizing 'Delivering insights, actions and outcomes,' with a diagram showing Observability at the center, connected to Networking, Security, and Applications. The scene transitions to a detailed slide labeled 'Cisco Full-Stack Observability use cases,' marked with a 'SHIPPING' banner, listing six use cases: Hybrid Application Monitoring, Modern (Cloud-native) Application Monitoring, Cost Insights + Application Resource Optimization, SAP Monitoring, Business Risk Observability, Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring, and AIOps. The camera then pulls back to reveal Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit with a white shirt and a red patterned pocket square, standing on a circular stage in a large, dimly lit auditorium. He gestures expressively while speaking, with his image projected on massive curved screens behind him. The audience is visible in the foreground, seated in rows under blue ambient lighting. The presentation continues with a new slide titled 'THE NEED,' stating 'Deliver the most optimal and secure experience for employees, partners and customers,' followed by 'Biggest Challenges'—Tool sprawl, High TCO, and Delayed resolution—each illustrated with icons. Ronak Desai walks across the stage, using hand gestures to emphasize points, as the camera alternates between wide shots of the stage and closer views of him. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner, reinforcing the event branding. The atmosphere is professional and high-tech, with dynamic blue stage lighting and large screens displaying the presentation content.

segments.caption: The video segment begins with a presentation slide titled 'Business Risk Observability,' announcing 'Data Security Posture Management (DSPM)' with a green 'ANNOUNCING' banner. The slide displays a dashboard interface for a 'Database Provider: Snowflake_US_East,' showing attributes like database type, cloud provider (AWS), region (US-EAST-1), and account details, alongside a table of alerts categorized by severity (Critical, Warning), status, category, start time, and alert rules. The slide emphasizes securing traditional and cloud-native applications and workloads, with bullet points for GenAI-powered data security, detecting sensitive data, discovering compliance violations, analyzing root cause, and taking actions. The scene transitions to another slide titled 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring,' marked with a yellow 'SHIPPING' banner, illustrating a flow from various digital touchpoints—Mobile, Browser/Desktop, Gaming apps; API Endpoint, Edge; Network/Internet; Multicloud Services/Apps; Data Center; SaaS Applications/Services—to 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring,' enabling 'Minutes to triage and recover.' A testimonial from First Abu Dhabi Bank highlights a reduction from 5 hours to less than 15 minutes for triage. The camera then cuts to a wide shot of a large auditorium with a circular stage, where a male speaker in a dark suit and white shirt, identified as Ronak Desai, stands holding a small device, gesturing as he presents. The stage is illuminated with blue lighting, and the audience is seated in rows. The presentation slides are displayed on large curved screens above the stage. The scene returns to the 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring' slide, then transitions to a new slide announcing 'Cisco Digital Experience Monitoring with Session Replay' and 'Browser Real User Monitoring (BRUM) and Mobile Real User Monitoring (MRUM),' featuring a graph of 'Requests per Minute' and 'End User Response Time Distribution' for a browser application named 'cloudkart-public.' The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner throughout the segment.

segments.caption: The segment opens with a close-up of Ronak Desai, a man with graying hair and a beard, wearing a dark suit with a white shirt and a red patterned pocket square, speaking on a dimly lit stage with a dark background. The camera pulls back to reveal a wide shot of the stage, showing Ronak standing on a circular platform in front of a large, dark audience illuminated by blue ambient lighting. Ronak continues to present, gesturing towards the screen, and the camera alternates between wide shots of the stage and audience, and medium shots of him speaking. The 'Cisco Live!' logo is consistently visible in the top right corner, and the overall environment maintains a professional, immersive, and tech-focused ambiance with dynamic stage lighting and a large, engaged audience.

segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of a man in a blue shirt and glasses, seated in a modern office setting, as the on-screen text reads 'to drive this transformation with a greater purpose.' The scene transitions to a woman in a green blazer speaking passionately in a minimalist room, with the text 'We are working with Cisco on our sustainability strategy' appearing. The camera then cuts to Umar Ahmed, Director, Global Head of Network Architecture at Deutsche Bank, speaking directly to the camera in a well-lit office, with the same sustainability text overlay. The scene shifts to a group of four professionals in a conference room with large windows, discussing around a table with laptops, as the text 'and, more importantly, increase our usage of renewable energy' is displayed. A man in a gray sweater is shown typing on a laptop outdoors, with a digital overlay of green foliage, reinforcing the sustainability theme. The video then shows a group of six people in a casual meeting on gray sofas, with the text 'the concept of smart buildings repeatable and sustainable.' Next, Colin Bannon, CTO of BT, speaks in a modern office, stating 'We’re looking at over 70 percent less electricity,' followed by a shot of him standing on a rooftop overlooking a cityscape, with the text 'And that’s better for our costs.' The scene transitions to a futuristic outdoor structure with solar panels, where a group of three people walk and converse, with the text 'Cisco is part of our family.' Eman Al Awadhi, Vice President, Network & Cyber Security at Expo City Technology, speaks in a modern office, with the text 'demonstrating our agility and commitment to a sustainable solution.' The video then cuts to an aerial view of a lush green island with a river and mountains, followed by a nighttime shot of a massive, illuminated Cisco-branded building. The final scenes show a large, dark auditorium with a circular stage, where a woman in a beige blazer speaks to a massive audience, with large screens displaying speaker profiles. The video concludes with a graphic showcasing six individuals from various companies, each with a sustainability-related achievement, such as 'Enabling a green future' and 'Efficient automated operations,' all under the 'Cisco Live!' branding.

segments.caption: The video opens with a wide shot of a large, dimly lit auditorium at Cisco Live!, where two men stand on a circular white stage in front of a massive audience. The man on the right, Oliver Tuszik, is animatedly speaking and gesturing with both hands, wearing a gray suit over a light blue shirt and sporting a full beard. The man on the left, Fletcher Previn, listens attentively, dressed in a dark suit and light blue shirt. The camera then pulls back to reveal the full scale of the venue, with a massive curved screen above the stage displaying Fletcher Previn’s name and title: 'SVP & Chief Information Officer'. The camera cuts to close-ups of Oliver Tuszik, who continues to speak with expressive hand movements and a warm smile, followed by Fletcher Previn, who begins to speak, his hands clasped in front of him. A lower-third graphic identifies Fletcher Previn as 'SVP and Chief Information Officer, Cisco'. The scene transitions to a detailed technical slide titled 'Deployment Architecture', which illustrates a complex network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)', showing various Cisco and SONiC components, including storage, compute, and management networks, with a legend identifying Cisco 8100 series, Nexus 9k, ISR 4k, and optical modules. The slide is branded with 'Cisco Live!' and includes a copyright notice for 2024.

segments.caption: The video begins with a detailed technical slide titled 'Deployment Architecture' from Cisco Live!, showcasing a complex network diagram for 'Mountain View (MTV)'. The diagram illustrates various network components including Storage Network (100Gb), In-band Network (100Gb), and OSM Network (10Gb), with specific Cisco and SONiC hardware like Cisco 8100 series, Nexus 9k, and ISR 4K, as well as Nvidia Optic modules. The slide includes a legend identifying different hardware and optical modules. The scene then transitions to a live stage presentation where two male hosts, one in a dark suit and the other in a gray suit, are standing on a dark stage with a large audience in the background. The host in the dark suit gestures expressively while speaking. The video then cuts to another slide titled 'Building a Cisco AI Cluster', displaying six images: Cisco 8000 series Silicon One devices with blue and yellow cables, an elevation view of AI cluster network fabrics, a front-side view of an Nvidia DGX compute server, a rear-side view of the same server with yellow cables, and Cisco's QDD-400G-DR4-S 400G optic providing network connectivity. The stage lighting is dark with blue and purple hues, and the Cisco Live! logo is visible in the top right corner throughout.
